raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I found a phone number through XXXX that claimed to be a free consultation line for the XXXX and XXXX regarding flight inquiries. I called the number to ask about my fathers flight. During the call, the person on the line told me that my fathers flight had been canceled and claimed that since I had not confirmed it, the cancellation was final. They said I needed to make a new payment to rebook the ticket. 

Believing this was legitimate, I provided my bank card information. However, I quickly realized that I had been scammed. As soon as I discovered this, I immediately filed a dispute online and also contacted the bank by phone to report the fraud. 

Today, I received an email from the bank stating that I would have to take full responsibility for the charge. I do not understand this decision. I reported the fraud right away and expected the bank to stop the transaction or at least protect me from the loss. Instead, I am being told that I must bear the cost myself. 

I am extremely dissatisfied with this outcome and request a clear explanation of why the bank did not intercept the fraudulent charge despite my immediate reporting, and why I am being held responsible for a situation that was clearly a scam.
